items.title: Officials seek to identify 3 sets of remains found in Oconee Co.

items.title: Rom Reddy holds press conference claiming attacks from opponents
items.description: (WSPA) -- Monday morning, a press conference was held, reportedly denouncing desperate attacks leveled against GOP gubernatorial candidate Rom Reddy, who says they are by "second-tier candidates and dark money groups." Reddy said he will unveil ethics reform legislation to end the dark money system in South Carolina.

items.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (WCBD) – Guests visiting South Carolina’s largest zoo can now meet one of the world’s most endangered primate species. Riverbanks Zoo and Garden recently welcomed a pair of cotton-top tamarins, named Pepita and Caqueta, to the Riverbanks Conservation Outpost. According to the zoo, Pepita arrived after being removed from an unsafe living situation [...]

items.title: South Carolina sees drop in gas prices
items.description: CHARLESTON, S.C. (WCBD) - Gas prices across South Carolina fell over the past week, offering some relief to drivers heading into the summer season. items.title: ‘Emergency ICU situation’: Upstate vet talks preventing algae poisoning in pets
items.description: GREENVILLE, S.C. (WSPA) – An Upstate veterinarian is putting pet owners on high alert about potential algae poisoning in pets.  Algae blooms are plant-like organisms, medical experts said, that produce dangerous toxins and can form in both deep waters and shallow ponds. That can include backyards as well as swimming pools that aren’t well-maintained.  Veterinarian [...]

items.description:
The 'NEW' Burrito Bowl at El Conquistador Mexican 2 is awesome! You can choose either grilled chicken or steak. I went with grilled chicken on my visit and it was delicious! The service during my visit was awesome too!

The Burrito Bowl is a new menu item available at all three El Conquistador locations. I went to their #2 location at 2401 Reidville Rd, in the plaza with the Food Lion grocery store. The Burrito Bowl comes with rice, black beans, corn, pico de gallo, lettuce, guacamole, cheese an

items.title: There's something special about a
items.description:
There's something special about a Saturday morning at the Hub City Farmers Market.

I had a chance to volunteer this weekend and was reminded how much heart our community has. From local growers and artisans to families enjoying the morning together, it was a wonderful way to spend the day.

Thank you to everyone who helps make events like this possible. 💚

#SpartanburgSC #HubCityFarmersMarket #CommunityConnection #SupportLocal #UpstateLiving

Happy National Trails Day! 🌳

Trails shape the way people move through and experience a community, creating space for connection, exploration, and everyday access to the outdoors. We’re grateful to help support spaces like these and the role they play in our communities.

What do you enjoy most when you’re on a trail? 🥾

items.title: OneSpartanburg, Inc. is not affiliated with any group or funding effort attempting to influence County Council elections...
items.description: OneSpartanburg, Inc. is not affiliated with any group or funding effort attempting to influence County Council elections. Claims by candidates that we are linked to spending behind recent mailers, and that we invited candidates to meet with our investors, are false. We do aim to educate our constituents and partners on local candidates’ views on economic issues. We invited candidates to speak to municipal leaders at our regularly scheduled Mayoral meeting, and invited candidates to respond to a

items.description: One year and ten days ago, Johnston Design Group and Edifice were hired to design and build a new stadium for Triumph SC. Three weeks later, graders were already on site. And less than a year after that — we were there for the first game, the first roar of the crowd, and the first victory in their new home. items.title: Early voting is winding down ahead of the June 9 Primary, with contested races for both Spartanburg County Council and S...
items.description: Early voting is winding down ahead of the June 9 Primary, with contested races for both Spartanburg County Council and SC House seats. OneSpartanburg, Inc. has sought to educate voters and our county’s business community on where candidates stand on important issues, as chambers of commerce across the country have done for hundreds of years. To learn more about where the candidates on June’s Primary ballot stand, head to https://www.onespartanburginc.com/vote.

items.title: New City/County Government Center
items.description: Steel is up to (essentially) roof level now!  We can begin to get a sense for the height/presence of the building.  Also, steel is finally going up on the north end of the building.  The parking deck has reached the central stair/elevator tower that will have pedestrian access to the interior plaza, and you can see the center car entrance on Spring too.

items.title: Every day, most of us reserve a little time for ourselves. A quiet cup of coffee. A walk. A moment to breathe. What if y...
items.description: Every day, most of us reserve a little time for ourselves. A quiet cup of coffee. A walk. A moment to breathe. What if your home did that for you — all the time? That's the idea behind Living Wellness in restorative design. Not wellness as a checklist, but as something built into the way a home is made: the quality of its light, the freshness of its air, its relationship to the land it sits on, the peace that comes from a space designed with you — your life, your rhythms, your wellbeing — in min

items.title: Rain? ✅ Mud? ✅ 24 straight hours of teamwork? ✅ From Friday night to Saturday night, our team took on the 24-Hour Relay ...

Rain? ✅ Mud? ✅ 24 straight hours of teamwork? ✅

#SWculture #SWpeople #relayrace

items.title: The City of Spartanburg will kick off Juneteenth Celebration Weekend with an artist talk by Spartanburg native Melvin L...
items.description: The City of Spartanburg will kick off Juneteenth Celebration Weekend with an artist talk by Spartanburg native Melvin L Nesbitt Jr., followed by a reception for his exhibition, The Get-Together: The Art of Melvin Nesbitt, Jr. Nesbitt spent much of his childhood in local public housing, an experience that continues to inspire his work today. Through vibrant mixed-media collages, he captures the joy, community, and everyday moments of Black life, offering powerful narratives that celebrate memory,

items.title: The Spartanburg area gained 400 jobs in March 2026 and has averaged approximately 255 new jobs per month over the past f...
items.description: The Spartanburg area gained 400 jobs in March 2026 and has averaged approximately 255 new jobs per month over the past five years, continuing a broader trend of workforce and economic growth across the region. Job growth impacts more than just businesses. It affects career opportunities, household stability, workforce development and long-term economic momentum across Spartanburg County. Growth conversations are also conversations about jobs, opportunity and the future of our community. Source:

items.description: On Tuesday, City Council unanimously approved first reading of a development agreement for a $71 million mixed-use development to be constructed on the former City Hall block at 145 W Broad St. The development will feature 220 market-rate apartments and nearly 15,000 sq. ft. of commercial space. The project will also wrap a nearly 1,000-space parking garage centered on the block that will serve the development and the new joint City-County local government facility. items.description: Good to hear about The Dan.  I've long thought we need to extend The Hopper by converting the remaining two blocks of Kennedy (between Converse and Church) to 3 lanes with a multi-use path on the north side (to match the section between Converse and Union).  This just confirms that need.  Hopefully we can get that going ASAP, so we don't have that 2 block gap for too long.  Kennedy is a state road, so it could be challenging.  But then again, they're moving quickly with East Main. Are you sure a

items.title: Spartanburg City Council approved first reading for the upcoming fiscal year budget at their meeting on Tuesday night. T...
items.description: Spartanburg City Council approved first reading for the upcoming fiscal year budget at their meeting on Tuesday night. The plan holds the property tax rate steady, keeps all fees unchanged, and channels revenue growth into essential public services. Budget projections show roughly five percent revenue growth over the current year's adopted budget, reflecting an expanding commercial tax base that is able to carry a larger share of the City's costs and reducing the tax burden on residential househ

items.title: Strong Towns Spartanburg is hosting a group bike ride called Pedal Burg 🌼! Join us at Fretwell on Wednesday, June 17th ...
items.description:
Strong Towns Spartanburg is hosting a group bike ride called Pedal Burg 🌼! Join us at Fretwell on Wednesday, June 17th at 6:30 pm

We'll start the ride at Fretwell, go through the route twice, and then end back at Fretwell. The ride takes roughly 15-20 minutes and we will be going at a leasurely pace. 
Children are more than welcome to join!

The reasons we are hosting a group bike ride:
1. There is safety in numbers. Having a bunch of people biking at the same time is generally safer around car

items.title: Twenty-five years. Same mission. When JDG opened its doors in Greenville in 2001, we believed architecture could do more...
items.description: Twenty-five years. Same mission. When JDG opened its doors in Greenville in 2001, we believed architecture could do more than shelter — it could restore. Restore well-being. Restore community. Restore our relationship with the places we inhabit. That belief has never wavered. And as we step into our next chapter, we're returning to it with fresh energy and deep gratitude for the Upstate community that has grown alongside us. Here's to the next 25 — together. #JDG #RestorativeDesign #Greenville #

items.description:
Welcome Kyle to our Charlotte office 🔥
 
As Survey Crew Chief, Kyle helps lead field operations through detailed data collection, property research, and equipment management. He brings hands-on surveying experience and a strong technical background, earning degrees from both Clemson University and Coastal Carolina University.
 
When he’s not in the field, you’ll probably find him trail riding dirt bikes or in the gym.
 
Excited to have you on #TeamSW, Kyle!

items.description:
Introducing Liam 👏
 
Liam is a graduate of Coastal Carolina University with a degree in Sustainability and Coastal Resilience. He is a Surveying Field Technician I on our Charlotte land survey team and focuses on precision, accuracy, and producing work that meets high standards.
 
Outside of work, he enjoys fishing, playing disc golf, hiking, and exploring new places.
 
Glad to have you on the team, Liam!

items.description:
🪏 The Town has officially broken ground on the Kenny Mile Trail! 🎉 

The Kenny Mile Trail is an approximately 1.25-mile multi-use path that will connect the existing Park West Trail system to Porcher’s Bluff Road, while also linking to the recently completed All American Boulevard shared-use path. Designed to improve safe, separated access for pedestrians and cyclists, the trail will run largely within the Mount Pleasant Waterworks easement and will be offset more than 60 feet from U.S. Highway

items.title: We're thrilled to join our friends at OneSpartanburg, Inc.  in welcoming Lepore Ristorante Italiano to Downtown Spartanb...
items.description: We're thrilled to join our friends at OneSpartanburg, Inc.  in welcoming Lepore Ristorante Italiano to Downtown Spartanburg! Lepore is open Tuesday through Saturday for lunch from 12 PM-2:30 PM and Tuesday through Saturday for dinner starting at 5 PM. Lepore offers scratch-made classic Italian cuisine and impeccable service in an upscale atmosphere. Be sure to check out their new location at 116 Magnolia Street!

items.title: Paddles up for a purpose. 🛶💛 Over the weekend, MPS Paddlers joined 24 teams at Prisma Health’s 20th anniversary Dragon...
items.description: Paddles up for a purpose. 🛶💛 Over the weekend, MPS Paddlers joined 24 teams at Prisma Health’s 20th anniversary Dragon Boat Upstate Festival on Lake Hartwell, raising funds to support local cancer patients and advance cancer research through the Prisma Health Cancer Institute’s Center for Integrative Oncology and Survivorship program. Teams took part in a mini parade, walking alongside cancer patients and in memory of loved ones who have been lost. It was a powerful reminder of why this event ma

items.title: OneSpartanburg, Inc.’s talent solutions team was thrilled to partner with beloved community institution Wade's Restauran...
items.description: OneSpartanburg, Inc.’s talent solutions team was thrilled to partner with beloved community institution Wade's Restaurant for the third-consecutive year to award the Wade’s Culture of Excellence Scholarship, dedicated to helping Wade’s team members continue their education. This year’s recipient, Louisa Sterritt, plans to attend the University of South Carolina Upstate. Congrats, Louisa and both our organization and Wade’s wish you the best as a Spartan! Wade's Jr.

items.title: Welcome Isabella Alamilla to SW+! 🎉 A Civil Designer II in our Charlotte office, Isabella brings experience in environm...
items.description:
Welcome Isabella Alamilla to SW+! 🎉

A Civil Designer II in our Charlotte office, Isabella brings experience in environmental engineering and land development, with a passion for residential projects and growing the Carolinas.

Outside of work, she enjoys live music, trying new restaurants, and volunteering at her local humane society. We’re excited to have her on the team! 👏

#SWpeople #newhire #CivilDesigner
